prompt: Can you give me a recipe for fried rice?
response:
This is a simple and delicious recipe for fried rice!

Ingredients: ½ cup vegetable oil, ½ cup chopped bell pepper, ¼ cup sliced mushrooms, 1 onion, diced small, ¼ teaspoon garlic cloves minced or pressed, juice of 1 lime or lemon half (optional), pinch of salt, cooked Asian-style long grain and thin-slices rice mixed with some cooking liquid from the rice, such as chicken stock or broth.

Instructions:
Heat the oil in a wok over medium heat. Add the bell pepper and mushrooms; stir-fry for about one minute. Add the onion; stir-fry for an additional two minutes until softened. Add the garlic; stir carefully so it does not brown too much. Remove from heat; transfer vegetables to a bowl and set aside.Add another tablespoon of oil to your wok if necessary over medium/high heat (oil should be almost smoking when added). If using lemon/lime juice, add now along with salt to taste – do not add more if it has already been added to chicken stock since there should be enough liquid from soaked/boiled rice that we have reintroduced into this recipe later on – then add cooked & drained rice quickly followed by any stray liquids still retained within it and mix everything together very well until browned slightly - this may require adding more vege/herb oil during this process as desired depending upon texture eaten preference at which time coolly remove potatoes from pot & discard skins in order to create crunch without them after rinsing thoroughly under cool running water when ready (this could even be frozen sweet plantings too).
